title: fix: split Veo pricing by resolution
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Splits Veo 3.1 Fast into fixed `veo` (720p) and `veo-1080p` tiers.
- Meters official Google rates exactly: $0.08/s or $0.10/s video plus $0.02/s only when audio is enabled.
- Locks upstream resolution to the selected model and records audio usage only for audio-enabled output.
- Uses capability-only model descriptions and updates OpenAPI source schemas without touching generated `APIDOCS.md`.
- Requires the model-management skill for model changes and makes automated review requests opt-in.
- Validated with Biome, TypeScript, docs checks, 281 focused tests, and real local-token E2E generations: 720p audio $0.40, 1080p audio $0.48, and 720p silent $0.32.

gist.headline: Every pixel gets its pollen
gist.blurb: Veo now knows whether it is making a 720p or 1080p movie, and bills the matching official Google rate. Audio is counted only when the soundtrack actually joins the picnic.
gist.summary: Split Veo 3.1 Fast into fixed 720p `veo` and 1080p `veo-1080p` tiers, locking each model to its selected upstream resolution and metering video at $0.08/s or $0.10/s plus $0.02/s only for audio-enabled output.
gist.impact: Video creators get resolution-specific Veo generation with accurately recorded audio usage, validated at $0.40 for 720p audio, $0.48 for 1080p audio, and $0.32 for 720p silent output.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy video garden has two clearly labeled film-projectors or retro monitors: one rendering a 720p flower meadow and one rendering a more detailed 1080p meadow, with separate little pollen-meter jars. Polly, wearing a tiny director beret and holding a clapboard, checks that the audio musical-note jar is connected only to the audio-enabled projector; code-plants and flowers make the technical accounting feel friendly.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a warm garden editing studio: the main character is a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body and dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks on cheeks, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings; she wears a tiny director beret and holds a clapboard while inspecting two retro video monitors labeled in simple pixel font “720p” and “1080p,” each with its own little lime green (#ecf874) pollen meter, while musical notes flow only into the audio-enabled monitor. Code-plants, flowering vines, and a small film reel surround her; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
